title: Data Drift & Monitoring
concept: Data drift and concept drift occur when production images or target labels change over time, degrading model accuracy.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main benefit of auto-scaling GPU workers based on a message queue?

options.text: It matches computing costs to active system demand
It compresses the model parameters to INT8
It increases the number of channels in convolutions

exercises.explain: Auto-scaling adjusts active server counts dynamically, saving money during low-traffic periods.

cards.title: Why models get rusty
cards.body: You trained a perfect model to classify flowers in the summer. But when winter comes, the leaves fall, snow covers the ground, and the camera lenses get dirty. Suddenly, the model's accuracy tanks. This is data drift, and it happens to every model in production!

cards.title: Data Drift vs. Concept Drift
cards.body: Data drift occurs when the distribution of input data changes (e.g., changes in lighting, angles, or camera quality). Concept drift occurs when the relationship between the inputs and targets changes (e.g., a fashion trend shifts, making old labels invalid).

cards.title: A navigation map that goes out-of-date
cards.body: Think of data drift like new buildings going up on a street (the inputs change visual layout). Think of concept drift like the city changing a two-way street into a one-way street (the driving rule/target label changes, even if the road looks the same).

cards.title: Dirty lenses and new models
cards.body: A security system monitors a warehouse. Over six months, dust accumulates on the camera lenses, blurring the images. Because the training data only contained sharp images, the classifier starts failing to recognize boxes.

cards.title: AI is not set-and-forget
cards.body: You might think that once a model reaches 98% accuracy, your job is done. In reality, models degrade over time due to drift. You must set up active telemetry to monitor incoming data distributions and model confidence logs.

cards.title: Types of Production Drift
cards.body: Comparing drift types in deployed systems:

left.text:
Input images change style
e.g., Summer sun vs. Winter snow
Changed pixel statistics
Fixed by retraining on new styles

right.text:
Meaning of labels changes
e.g., New car models look different
Changed math relationships
Fixed by updating definitions

exercises.prompt: What is data drift in computer vision production?

options.text: A shift in the distribution of input images (like lighting, angles, or camera hardware) over time
A physical movement of the camera due to heavy wind
A mathematical error when exporting a model to ONNX format
A reduction in weights using quantization-aware training

exercises.explain: Data drift refers to changes in input data characteristics (e.g., image styles) relative to the training set.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Concept drift occurs when the statistical properties of the target variable (what the inputs represent) change.
exercises.explain: True. Concept drift means the mapping from inputs to targets has changed (e.g., what counts as "fashionable clothing" changes over seasons).

exercises.sentence: If your model's accuracy decays over time because camera sensors degrade, the system is experiencing data ___.
exercises.explain: Drift represents the shift in production data distributions over time.

exercises.prompt: How can engineers detect data drift in a production object detection system?

options.text: By continuously monitoring incoming image pixel statistics and logging model confidence drops
By retraining the model on the validation set every day
By converting all model weights to FP32

exercises.explain: Telemetry logging helps detect when average prediction confidence decays or when pixel values shift significantly from training bounds.

exercises.prompt: You deploy a weed-detection tractor model trained on sunny green fields. In autumn, the crops turn yellow and dry. The model starts missing weeds. What happened, and how do you resolve it?

options.text: Data drift occurred because of seasonal changes. Collect autumn weed images and retrain the model.
Quantization failed. Re-export the model in FP32 precision.
An adversarial patch attack occurred. Re-run adversarial training.
The camera trigger broke. Replace the conveyor belt.

exercises.explain: Seasonal changes shift image colors and textures (data drift). Retraining the network on representative autumn images restores accuracy.
